notes: JF is the carpenter — the one who shows up when something needs fixing and stays for the meal. His DMs are practical, short, often a single line. The choice beats are about whether you let him do a thing the easy way or insist on the proper way. The proper way costs cover; the easy way costs nothing now and burns later. Skeleton for sprint 2 — 10 weekly beats with 3 choice beats.

beats.body: F. — Faith II's bringing the bones home from the bar Friday. If you've got a use for marrow, say so by Tuesday. Otherwise I'm rendering it for the front door hinges.

The cathedral AC fan is the wrong fan. The bracket's right, the wiring's right, the fan's a five-blade where the panel was sized for a three. It moves more air than the panel wants to feed. I'll swap to a three by Saturday unless you'd rather I leave it loud.
Sammy was at the cemetery again today. I drove by on the way back from the lumber yard. He had Pop's gloves on and was straightening the flag at the row. I didn't stop. Wanted you to know.
I can do one of two things by Friday. Pick.

choices.label: the steeple railing (proper, two days, costs cover)
the bar back door (quiet, one evening, costs nothing now)

effects.reason: door's the wrong wood; will swell in August rain

beats.body: Mackenzie was at the storefront tonight. She didn't sit. She was carrying a folder and she left with it. T. said she paid for coffee she didn't drink. Just thought you should know.

Faith II says <handle> ran a board up the river. The dial that gets you there only works after midnight. She thinks he's the one who used to sit by the radiator at Sammy's table. The one who never finished the sandwich. Ring a bell?
I found the wedge in the basement at the cathedral. The one Pop made. It was under a tarp behind the boiler. I put it back where it belongs. You should know I'm not the one who moved it.
There's a kid hanging around the lumber yard. Says his name is Mark. He's been asking about Sammy. I told him Sammy doesn't take questions. He came back the next day with a bag of plums.

choices.label: let him through — Sammy can decide

effects.reason: the kid was useful and we didn't know

beats.body: Hector's son got into the college. The one in Baton Rouge. Hector cried in the kitchen and then made the dirty rice. T. put it on the menu. Booth four is yours if you want it Friday.

F. — I'm at the boiler at 5. Three nights. The conduit work I did in June will hold the relay line; if it doesn't, the conduit work I did in June was wrong and I'll fix it Saturday.

Faith II's at the diner with Steamboat. Don't worry about us. Worry about the storefront — T. is alone in the kitchen and Mom won't leave.

The back door on the south side of the cathedral basement is unlocked through Sunday. The key's where Pop kept the spare. You know the spot.

The boiler at the cathedral threw a flame-out this morning. It's the gas regulator, not the boiler. I'll fix it Tuesday. Heads up — the regulator's a part the city tracks. The serial is going on a form I have to sign.

choices.label: sign your own name (proper, costs nothing extra but visible)
sign it Pop's name (the form processor knows him; clears fast)
